title: Take my life, and let it be (revised)
firstLine: Take my life, and let it be
publicDomainBasis: Frances Ridley Havergal (1836–1879). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Take my life, and let it be
Consecrated, Lord, to Thee;
Take my moments and my days,
Let them flow in ceaseless praise.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
Take my hands and feet, I pray,
For Thy work, to run Thy way.
Take my lips that I may sound
Word of truth to those around.

sections.label: Chorus
Chorus
sections.text:
Make my heart and will Thine own;
They belong to Thee alone.
Take my love; my Lord, I pour
At Thy feet its treasure-store.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
Take me, Lord, till I’ve become
Ever joined to Thee as one.
Take myself, and I will be
Ever, only, all for Thee.
